title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Optional chaining (?.) safely accesses nested object properties without throwing a TypeError if an intermediate value is null or undefined — it just returns undefined instead.

Variants:
• obj?.prop — property access.
• obj?.[expr] — computed property access.
• func?.() — function call (only calls if func is not null/undefined).

Couple with nullish coalescing (??) to provide a default value.
